records.evidence: README.md:11-14 — operational gotcha with the extract tells verbatim ('One thing worth knowing', 'silently binds a random port') addressed to whoever operates the dev server, sitting in the user-facing README Usage section; the gotcha sentence begins mid-line 11 after `Run \`exporter run --tenant NAME\`.`, which must stay — only the gotcha moves. `grep -rn portlock` matches only README.md:14, so no agent/operator doc carries the `.portlock` fact today; CLAUDE.md's Gotchas section is its home.

proposal.text: - The dev server keeps a `.portlock` file after a crash and silently binds a random port on the next start — delete `.portlock` before restarting.

records.evidence: README.md:16-18 — caveat tell verbatim ('Note that') on platform-specific export behavior, addressed to anyone diffing exports across platforms, sitting in the user-facing README. The right doc already exists: docs/reference/export.md:4-5 states 'Newline handling is explicit: the writer opens files in binary mode (`src/export.py:4`)' but lacks the Windows trailing-newline consequence this passage carries; src/export.py:4 comment 'opens in binary mode to control newlines explicitly' backs the mechanism.

proposal.text: - Windows builds drop the trailing newline (binary-mode writer, `src/export.py:4`), so exports diff by one line-ending across platforms.
